notes: Both disagreement entries (enam/ayam scope; vijānītaḥ technical sense) are substantive. The first explains the Advaita / Vishishtadvaita divergence carefully (Advaita: the Self is not a doer at all; Vishishtadvaita: the jīva is preserved but is not the bodily-act doer) and notes that BOTH readings agree that 'I killed Bhīṣma' is metaphysically wrong — they disagree on whose 'I' is doing the wrong attribution. This is genuinely useful pedagogy. The second explains viveka (discriminative knowledge) as the technical sense of vijānīta. Both exceed 200 chars and contain real reasoning. PASS.
